steps.title: Identify the sentence's main verb
steps.content: The sentence has one main action: “later changed.” The blank must describe what happened to the codes beforehand without introducing another main verb in this sentence.

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining verbs in one sentence
steps.content: The past participle “assigned” functions as a modifier rather than as a main verb, allowing “changed” to be the sentence's only main verb. Inserting any finite verb form (were assigned, had been assigned, or would be assigned) would create two main verbs in the same sentence without a comma-and-conjunction, semicolon, or period separating them—a run-on error. That matches choice C — C is correct.
